gates.automation_boundary: Ask per phase; if interactive question tools are unavailable, present plain-text choices and stop for user input unless an explicit safe auto/default flag applies.
gates.description: Selects Manual Questions or Trusted Self-Questioning before any discuss-stage analysis, questions, checkpoints, or canonical artifact writes. workflow.discuss_mode is routing only, not approval evidence.
gates.name: Discuss Mode Gate
observable_outputs: Gate Resolution entry naming selected mode, approval source, and evidence
Gate Resolution entry naming cycle limits or skip reason
CONTEXT.md discuss mode or gate resolution block

gates.automation_boundary: Fetch or pull only with explicit permission when network or remote mutation policy requires it; use upstream as design input, not as automatic authority.
gates.description: Checks the local ai-workspace-kit reference against reachable upstream before planning or major replanning.
gates.name: ai-workspace-kit Upstream Freshness Gate
required_artifacts: active phase RESEARCH.md or PLAN.md Gate Resolution

gates.automation_boundary: If the need is kit-owned, create an outbox request instead of implementing it in AI Tools.
gates.description: Prevents AI Tools from implementing functionality owned by ai-workspace-kit.
gates.name: Cross-Repo Outgoing Need Gate
observable_outputs: outbox capability request when boundary belongs to ai-workspace-kit
explicit skip reason when no outgoing need exists
required_artifacts: active RESEARCH.md or PLAN.md Gate Resolution

gates.automation_boundary: Incoming requests create decision points only; they do not automatically create phases, run tools, add dependencies, or copy planning state.
gates.description: Reviews incoming cross-repo capability requests for roadmap impact and boundary decisions.
gates.name: Cross-Repo Incoming Review Gate
observable_outputs: decision artifact or explicit no-incoming-request note

gates.automation_boundary: Changelog entries are planning evidence, not approval to consume changed capabilities automatically.
gates.description: Requires concise changelog entries after completed phases, major tasks, or workflow gate changes.
gates.name: Project Changelog Gate
observable_outputs: CHANGELOG.md entry with changed scope, validation, upstream impact, compatibility, breaking changes, and migration notes
required_artifacts: SUMMARY.md or VERIFICATION.md Gate Resolution when relevant

gates.automation_boundary: Run validated matching AI Tools as read-only evidence; tool output never replaces assistant judgment.
gates.description: Checks whether AI Tools should use its own validated capabilities while developing this repository.
gates.name: AI Tools Self-Use Gate
observable_outputs: command and output location when run
skip reason when no validated matching tool exists
required_artifacts: review packet output when a validated self-use tool runs

gates.automation_boundary: Do not implement a new tool until owner, destination, maturity, activation stage, outputs, and non-goals are explicit.
gates.description: Routes new AI Tools capability ideas to the right owner and destination before implementation.
gates.name: New Tool Intake and Placement Gate

gates.automation_boundary: Do not silently mix baseline files with active work; broad baseline staging needs explicit approval.
gates.description: Separates existing baseline/project files from active changes before using git cleanliness as evidence.
gates.name: Git Baseline Gate
observable_outputs: unresolved baseline noise in STATE.md when present

gates.automation_boundary: Use future ai-workspace-kit gate review only when it exists; until then, review manually and route findings through requests or decisions.
gates.description: Reviews gates for conflicts, staleness, duplication, relevance, and dependency creep at maintenance boundaries.
gates.name: Future ai-workspace-kit Gate Review Hook
observable_outputs: manual gate review notes or future review packet
decision or request artifacts for required changes
explicit skip reason when upstream capability is unavailable
